title: Remote Graphic Designer Jobs at Tech Companies (Canada)
description: Find the latest remote graphic designer positions at top Canadian tech companies like Shopify, Wealthsimple, and 1Password. Real roles, real CAD salary ranges.

mainEntity.name: Do Canadian tech companies pay graphic designers well?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. While traditional design agencies often pay $45,000–$65,000 CAD, tech companies (like Shopify, Wealthsimple, or 1Password) typically offer much higher compensation. Mid-level graphic designers at these companies often see salaries ranging from $75,000 to $110,000 CAD, alongside stock options (RSUs or ISOs) and strong benefits.

mainEntity.name: Is remote work still common for designers in tech?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, especially in Canada. Many top Canadian tech companies have adopted 'digital by default' or highly flexible hybrid models. This means you can often work from anywhere in Canada (including Montreal or the South Shore) while earning a top-tier salary.

mainEntity.name: What skills do tech companies look for beyond just visual design?

acceptedAnswer.text: Tech companies heavily value cross-functional collaboration. You'll need to demonstrate experience working with marketing teams, copywriters, and sometimes product designers. Proficiency in Figma, understanding of web accessibility, animation/motion design (After Effects), and basic understanding of conversion rate optimization (CRO) are massive advantages.

mainEntity.name: How is a 'Brand Designer' different from a 'Graphic Designer' in tech?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the tech sector, 'Brand Designer' is often the modern evolution of the graphic designer role. Brand designers focus on holistic visual identity, marketing websites, campaign assets, and design systems, whereas product designers (UX/UI) focus on the software application itself. Transitioning your title to Brand Designer can often unlock higher salary bands.
